title: Atomic Habits
summary_en: A practical guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones.
summary_zh: 一本关于养成好习惯和打破坏习惯的实用指南。
best_quote_en: You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.
best_quote_zh: 你不会达到目标的高度，而是会跌落到系统的水平。

title: Cancel Culture Comes for Artists Who Posted About Charlie Kirk’s Death
title_zh: “取消文化” 波及悼念查理・柯克之死的艺术家
summary_en: After right-wing influencer Charlie Kirk, an anti-trans rights ally of Trump, was shot dead during his American Comeback Tour, several artists and media figures faced professional repercussions for mocking or making controversial comments about his death. Gretchen Felker-Martin, writer of DC Comics' Red Hood series, was fired by DC and had her Bluesky account suspended for posts like 'thoughts and prayers you Nazi bitch'; Drew Harrison, an artist at Sucker Punch Productions, was fired for a post about the shooter; MSNBC commentator Matthew Dowd was dismissed by Comcast over an 'unacceptable and insensitive comment'. Additionally, Comedy Central pulled the South Park episode 'Got a Nut' that satirized Kirk from reruns (though it remains on Paramount+). DC stated the issue was Felker-Martin's post tone, not her views, while Bluesky cited 'violent or threatening speech' for the suspension. Critics like Roxane Gay argued this was an overreaction and a threat to free speech, and curator Hrag Vartanian noted artists are self-censoring due to the political climate. Felker-Martin, despite receiving death threats, found comfort in queer community gatherings.
summary_zh: 右翼影响者、反跨性别权利的特朗普盟友查理・柯克在其 “美国复兴巡回” 活动中遭枪击身亡后，多名艺术家和媒体人士因嘲讽其死亡或发表争议性评论而面临职业后果。DC 漫画《红头罩》系列编剧格雷琴・费尔克 - 马丁因发布 “为你这纳粹贱人献上思念与祈祷” 等言论，被 DC 解雇且 Bluesky 账号被暂停；Sucker Punch Productions（索尼旗下子公司）的艺术家德鲁・哈里森因发布关于枪手的言论被解雇；MSNBC 评论员马修・多德因 “不可接受且麻木不仁的评论” 被康卡斯特解雇。此外，喜剧中心将讽刺柯克的《南方公园》剧集《Got a Nut》下架（但仍可在派拉蒙 + 流媒体平台观看）。DC 称问题在于费尔克 - 马丁的发帖语气而非其观点，Bluesky 则以 “暴力或威胁性言论” 为由暂停其账号。罗克珊・盖伊等批评者认为这是过度反应，且威胁言论自由，策展人赫拉格・瓦尔塔尼安指出，受当前政治环境影响，艺术家正进行自我审查。尽管收到死亡威胁，费尔克 - 马丁仍从酷儿社群聚会中获得慰藉。
best_quote_en: Either you believe in free speech or you don't. — Roxane Gay
best_quote_zh: 要么你信奉言论自由，要么你就不信。—— 罗克珊・盖伊

title: Why We Should Tune Into the Orchestra of the Animal World
title_zh: 为何我们应聆听动物世界的 “交响乐”
summary_en: This essay argues that the sounds of the animal world, described as a 'great animal orchestra', possess profound healing properties and connect humans to the broader web of life. It explores how various animal sounds—from birdsong and bee buzzing to cat purrs and dolphin frequencies—contribute to human mental and physical wellbeing. The text cites scientific studies (e.g., King’s College London research on birdsong improving mental health) and cultural examples (e.g., Navajo 'Singers', ancient Egyptian bee teachings, Slovenian bee-healing traditions) to support the idea that animal sounds restore harmony, reduce anxiety, and remind humans of their place as one species among many. It also highlights figures like soundscape ecologist Bernie Krause, who recorded natural soundscapes to emphasize the'sonic niche' of each creature, and notes how even infrasonic sounds (e.g., elephants, giraffes) and ultrasonic sounds (e.g., bats) shape this ecological symphony. Additionally, the essay touches on the infectious joy of animal laughter (e.g., bonobos, kea parrots) and the lessons humans learn from animals about embodied vitality, contrasting comforting animal sounds with unsettling ones to underscore the full spectrum of nature’s acoustic influence.
summary_zh: 本文认为，被描述为 “伟大动物交响乐” 的动物世界之声具有深远的疗愈作用，并能将人类与更广阔的生命网络相连。文章探讨了从鸟鸣、蜜蜂嗡嗡声到猫咕噜声、海豚发声频率等各类动物声音如何助力人类身心健康。文中引用科学研究（如伦敦国王学院关于鸟鸣改善心理健康的研究）和文化案例（如纳瓦霍族的 “歌者”、古埃及的蜜蜂教义、斯洛文尼亚的蜜蜂疗愈传统），佐证动物声音可恢复和谐、缓解焦虑，并提醒人类自身仅是众多物种中的一员。文章还重点介绍了声音景观生态学家伯尼・克劳斯等人物 —— 他通过录制自然声音景观，强调每种生物的 “声音生态位”，并指出即使是次声波（如大象、长颈鹿的声音）和超声波（如蝙蝠的声音）也在塑造这一生态交响乐。此外，本文还提及动物笑声（如倭黑猩猩、啄羊鹦鹉的笑声）所传递的感染力，以及人类从动物身上学到的关于躯体活力的启示；同时对比了令人安心与令人不安的动物声音，以凸显自然界声音影响的完整范围。
best_quote_en: “Through the sounds of nature, we may move into the fullness of what it is to be a person... A person is an instrument of listening, played by the everything that surrounds them, resonating with goat-song and puffin-joke and camel-carol. Our bodies rung by the wild voices, the bells, blowings and buglings of life, conducting us to that place both electric and tender where we can feel most fully alive.”
best_quote_zh: “透过自然之声，我们或许能体悟到作为人的完整意义…… 人是聆听的乐器，被周遭万物所奏响，与山羊的鸣叫、海鹦的嬉语、骆驼的欢歌共振。生命的野性呼唤、清脆铃音、悠长呼啸与洪亮号角萦绕我们的身躯，引领我们抵达一个既激昂又温柔的境地 —— 在那里，我们能最真切地感受到生命的蓬勃。”

title: The Cats are On To Something
summary_en: This linkpost discusses the unique relationship between humans and cats, framing it as a successful case of 'alignment' orchestrated by 'cat evolution' (dubbed the 'cat shoggoth') over nearly 5,000 years, dating back to Stone Age Egypt. It contrasts cats’ domestication with that of pigs or chickens, noting that cats retained their 'cat-ness' while getting humans to optimize for their dignity and wellbeing—unlike other livestock that suffer for human use. The author argues that this feline-human alignment has a key property: it scales with human intelligence, as advances in moral reasoning have improved cat welfare, and technology (e.g., laser toys, cat MRI machines) has been used to benefit cats. The core proposal is that since aligning AI to humans is extremely difficult, researchers should replicate the cat alignment process to build a self-improving AI that values cats. This would involve sequencing pre-domestic cat genomes, simulating ancient cat environments, and studying how the AI learns the 'cat utility function'—a process the author claims is feasible (given its natural precedent). The essay also suggests this AI could serve as a contingency against harmful AIs (e.g., paperclip maximizers) and mentions plans for 'city-sized cat heavens' as a potential outcome, with a playful note about wanting control over activating this 'catpocalypse'.
summary_zh: 这篇链接文章探讨了人类与猫咪之间独特的关系，将其视为近 5000 年前（可追溯至石器时代的埃及）由 “猫咪进化”（被称为 “猫咪修格斯”）精心策划的一次成功 “对齐” 案例。文章将猫咪的驯化与猪、鸡的驯化进行对比，指出猫咪在让人类为其尊严和福祉进行优化的同时，保留了自身的 “猫性”—— 这与为人类所用而承受痛苦的其他家畜不同。作者认为，这种猫与人类的对齐具有一个关键特性：它随人类智力的发展而发展 —— 道德推理的进步改善了猫咪的福利，而技术（如激光玩具、猫咪核磁共振仪）也被用于造福猫咪。文章的核心提议是，鉴于将人工智能（AI）与人类对齐极为困难，研究人员应复制猫咪对齐的过程，构建一个重视猫咪的自我改进型 AI。这需要对驯化前的猫咪基因组进行测序、模拟古代猫咪的生存环境，并研究 AI 如何学习 “猫咪效用函数”—— 作者称这一过程具有可行性（因其有自然先例）。文章还提出，这种 AI 可作为应对有害 AI（如回形针最大化器）的应急方案，并提及 “城市规模的猫咪天堂” 作为潜在成果，同时以戏谑的语气表示希望掌控启动这场 “猫咪天启” 的权力。
best_quote_en: “Aligning an unbounded, recursively improving intelligence to cats is boundedly hard because it’s already been done once. Copying the cat shoggoth’s homework should therefore be an absolute maximum priority task. We need to build a consequentialist, self improving reasoning model that loves cats.”
best_quote_zh: “将一个无边界、可递归自我改进的智能体与猫咪对齐，其难度是有限的 —— 因为这事儿已经成功过一次了。因此，复制‘猫咪修格斯’的‘作业’理应成为绝对的优先任务。我们需要构建一个遵循结果主义、能自我改进且热爱猫咪的推理模型。”

title: 科技爱好者周刊 (第 364 期): 最难还原的魔方
title_zh: 科技爱好者周刊（第 364 期）：最难还原的魔方
summary_en:
This is the 364th issue of Ruan Yifeng's Technology Enthusiasts Weekly, released on September 5, 2025. It mainly shares valuable technology-related content, covering multiple sections. The cover story focuses on the 'hardest-to-solve Rubik's Cube', introducing six criteria for defining the hardest-to-solve Rubik's Cube (e.g., each face has 6 colors, no more than 2 blocks of the same color per face, no consecutive same-color blocks on a face) and mentions that a foreign researcher used a C++ program to calculate all 43,252,003,274,489,856,000 Rubik's Cube permutations in 5 days, finally finding one permutation that meets all criteria (with 48 possible forms due to random face combinations). 
In the 'Software of the Week' section, it recommends GeeLark, an AI assistant for cross-border marketing, which supports one-stop management of overseas social media platforms (Facebook, Instagram, X/Twitter, etc.), with built-in cloud phones and fingerprint browsers, and new features like AI large models (for video generation, copywriting), TikTok automation, Reddit automation, custom tasks, and open APIs. 
The 'Technology Trends' section includes 5 news items: South Korea's 'holographic police' in Seoul parks reducing crime rates by ~22%, an American programmer modifying an old coffee machine into a functional 'coffee machine computer', Meta's wristband that controls computers via muscle electrical signals, a British company planning to convert a 1400-meter-deep abandoned mine in Finland into an energy storage battery, and Google Maps' new navigation ads. 
The 'Articles' section shares 6 technical articles (e.g., Cloudflare's crawler authentication, working as an engineer at Anduril, replacing caches with databases). The 'Tools' section recommends 10 tools (e.g., sping - enhanced ping, Beszel - lightweight server monitoring, WinApps - running Windows apps on Linux). The 'AI - Related' section introduces 4 AI applications (e.g., book - splitting AI summary tool, VIVY - Stable Diffusion desktop client). The 'Resources' section includes 3 resources (e.g., OpenAnimation - Lottie animation collection, Jujutsu introductory tutorial). The 'Images' section shows photos of instrument interiors. The 'Abstracts' section explains why Facebook struggles to retain scientists. The 'Quotes' section shares 5 viewpoints on AI's impact on employment, AI application risks, etc.
summary_zh:
这是阮一峰《科技爱好者周刊》的第 364 期，于 2025 年 9 月 5 日发布，主要分享有价值的科技相关内容，涵盖多个板块。封面主题为 “最难还原的魔方”，介绍了定义最难还原魔方的 6 个标准（如每面含 6 种颜色、每面同色色块不超过 2 个、面上无同色连续色块等），并提及国外有研究者用 C++ 程序计算魔方所有 43252003274489856000 种排列方式，耗时 5 天最终找到一种符合所有标准的排列（因面的随机组合，共存在 48 种形态）。
 “本周软件” 板块推荐跨境营销 AI 助理 GeeLark，支持一站式管理境外社媒平台（Facebook、Instagram、X/Twitter 等），内置云手机与指纹浏览器，新增 AI 大模型（用于视频生成、文案撰写）、TikTok 自动化、Reddit 自动化、自定义任务、开放 API 等功能。
 “科技动态” 板块包含 5 条资讯：韩国在首尔公园启用 “全息警察” 使犯罪率下降约 22%、美国程序员将老式咖啡机改装为可正常使用的 “咖啡机电脑”、Meta 公司推出通过肌肉电信号控制电脑的腕带、英国公司计划将芬兰 1400 米深废弃矿井改造成储能电池、谷歌地图新增导航广告。
 “文章” 板块分享 6 篇技术文章（如 Cloudflare 的爬虫认证、在 Anduril 当工程师、用数据库替换缓存等）；“工具” 板块推荐 10 款工具（如 sping 增强版 ping、Beszel 轻量级服务器监控、WinApps 在 Linux 运行 Windows 应用等）；“AI 相关” 板块介绍 4 款 AI 应用（如拆书 AI 总结工具、VIVY 的 Stable Diffusion 桌面客户端等）；“资源” 板块包含 3 类资源（如 OpenAnimation 的 Lottie 动画收集、Jujutsu 入门教程等）；“图片” 板块展示乐器内部照片；“文摘” 板块解析 Facebook 留不住科学家的原因；“言论” 板块分享 5 条关于 AI 对就业冲击、AI 应用风险等观点。
best_quote_en: “Different Rubik's cubes have different solving difficulties, but how to measure the difficulty? Recently, I accidentally found that this problem has long been studied abroad. Moreover, he proposed measurement criteria: the hardest-to-solve Rubik's cube should meet the following 6 conditions at the same time... He wrote a C++ program to find the hardest-to-solve Rubik's cube. There are 43,252,003,274,489,856,000 permutations of the Rubik's cube, and his home computer took 5 days to complete all calculations. Finally, it was found that only the following permutation meets the above six requirements.”
best_quote_zh: “不同的魔方，还原难度肯定是不一样的，但是怎么衡量难度呢？最近，我意外发现，这个问题在国外早有人研究了。而且，他提出了衡量标准：最难还原的魔方应该同时满足下面 6 个条件…… 他写了一个 C++ 程序，去找出最难还原的魔方。魔方的排列方式共有 43252003274489856000 种，他的家用计算机花了 5 天，才跑完所有计算。最终发现，只有下面一种排列，满足上面六个要求。”

title: Dark Age Stylesheet
summary_en:
This article introduces a visual design style guide created by the author with the help of ChatGPT, inspired by James Gunn's Superman style and infused with more 'Guardians of the Galaxy' energy. The guide is designed for the author's nonfiction writing, aiming to be pragmatic, realistic, emotionally positive, and able to accommodate the 'darkness of the current zeitgeist' without naivety. 
The author, who acknowledges limitations in independent design development, shares the original prompt given to ChatGPT (requesting color swatches, line work tone guidelines, principles for abstract/representational illustrations, and a stance on illustration types like literal vs metaphoric). ChatGPT generated three distinct styles: 
1. Optimistic Grit: Features a palette of Deep Navy (#1C3D5A), Muted Crimson (#8B3A3A), etc.; uses bold sans-serif (display) and humanist serif (body) typography with a slightly weathered tone; illustration line work is strong but imperfect, focusing on metaphorical rather than literal interpretations (e.g., a retro-textured Mars rover poster). 
2. Luminous Mischief: Uses Electric Blue (#1E3AFF), Magenta (#D726FF), etc.; adopts playful rounded sans (display) and clean sans (body) typography; illustrations have curvy, kinetic line work, blending literal and metaphoric elements with a mischievous yet resilient tone (e.g., a neon-cosmic Mars rover scene). 
3. Stoic Radiance: Includes Midnight Blue (#191970), Burgundy (#800020), etc.; uses monumental serif (display) and geometric sans (body) typography; illustrations feature clean, architectural line work and emblematic, heroic imagery (e.g., a printmaking-style humanoid rover poster). 
The article also details the creation process (direction setting → concept-image prototyping → style guide assembly) and deliverables (Markdown file, swatches image, multi-version PDFs) for project use, and invites feedback from professional designers due to the author's self-acknowledged design limitations. Additionally, the author mentions generating comp images for a potential future essay titled 'Mars Missions for a Political Dark Age'.
summary_zh:
本文介绍了作者借助 ChatGPT 生成的一份视觉设计风格指南，该指南灵感源自詹姆斯・古恩（James Gunn）的《超人》风格，并融入了更多《银河护卫队》（Guardians of the Galaxy）的风格元素。指南专为作者的非虚构写作设计，力求务实、真实、情感积极，同时能包容 “当下时代精神中的黑暗面”，且不失天真。
 作者坦言自身独立开发设计风格的能力有限，因此分享了给 ChatGPT 的原始提示词（要求包含色板、线条风格指南、抽象 / 具象插图原则，以及对插图类型的立场，如写实与隐喻的侧重等）。ChatGPT 最终生成了三种截然不同的风格：
1. 乐观坚韧（Optimistic Grit）：配色包括深蓝（#1C3D5A）、暗 crimson（#8B3A3A）等；排版采用粗体无衬线字体（标题）和人文主义衬线字体（正文），带有轻微复古质感；插图线条有力但不完美，侧重隐喻而非写实（示例：复古质感的火星车海报）。
2. 明亮俏皮（Luminous Mischief）：配色包括电蓝（#1E3AFF）、品红（#D726FF）等；排版采用活泼的圆体无衬线字体（标题）和简洁无衬线字体（正文）；插图线条流畅且富有动感，融合写实与隐喻元素，风格俏皮却不失韧性（示例：霓虹宇宙风的火星车场景）。
3. 沉稳闪耀（Stoic Radiance）：配色包括午夜蓝（#191970）、酒红（#800020）等；排版采用庄重的衬线字体（标题）和几何无衬线字体（正文）；插图线条简洁且具建筑感，图像象征意义强、充满英雄感（示例：版画风格的类人火星车海报）。
 文章还详细说明了风格指南的创作流程（方向设定→概念 - 图像原型制作→风格指南整合）及项目可用的交付物（Markdown 文件、色板图、多版本 PDF），并因作者自认设计能力有限，邀请专业设计师提供反馈。此外，作者提及已为潜在的未来文章《政治黑暗时代的火星任务》（Mars Missions for a Political Dark Age）生成了样图。
best_quote_en: “I want a visual design style guide for my writing work inspired by the James Gunn style as discussed in our recent chat. But with proportionately more of the guardians of the galaxy energy modifying the Superman energy. And suitable for my nonfiction writing or pragmatic and realistic without naïveté but emotionally positive and accommodating of real world darkness of current zeitgeist.”
best_quote_zh: “我想要一份为我的写作作品设计的视觉风格指南，灵感源自我们近期聊天中讨论的詹姆斯・古恩风格。但要融入更多《银河护卫队》的风格元素，以调整《超人》的风格基调。这份指南需适用于我的非虚构写作，要务实、真实，不失天真却情感积极，且能包容当下时代精神中的现实黑暗面。”

title: DOGE Days
summary_en:
This article is a first-person narrative by Sahil Lavingia, documenting his experience as a software engineer at the United States DOGE Service (formerly the United States Digital Service, renamed and reorganized via President Trump's executive order in January 2025) within the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) from March 17 to May 9, 2025. 
Before joining (Day 0), he completed fingerprinting at the Manhattan VA Medical Center, driven by a desire to code for the federal government—this motivation stemmed from his 2016 experience writing Google Apps code for Bernie Sanders' campaign, where he noticed the need for better tools for voter outreach. 
On Day 1, he was sworn in at the VA Central Office in DC, learning his title was Senior Advisor to the Chief of Staff with a $0 salary. His first task was reviewing VA's over 90,000 active contracts (covering IT support to landscaping). To streamline the manual, tedious review process, he proposed an LLM-powered script: extract text from contract PDFs, flag potential cancellations, compile results into a CSV, and build a dashboard for contracting officers. 
Key challenges emerged on Day 2: government software development relies heavily on contractors (IBM, Accenture, etc.) because restricted government laptops (due to security policies) prevent installing essential tools like Git, Python, or Cursor—fixing this root issue would require congressional intervention, making outsourcing more practical. 
On Day 3, he collaborated on Reduction in Force (RIF) planning, built an MVP org chart visualizer using VA's consolidated HR data (in IBM's HR Smart), and learned RIF rules prioritize tenure (new hires first), veterans' preference, and seniority over performance (a stark contrast to private industry layoffs). 
He later discovered VA's ambitious projects (e.g., cutting veterans' benefits claims processing from 133 days to under a week) and open-source efforts (e.g., the 40-year-old VistA electronic health record system). With VA's CTO's advice, he resolved laptop restrictions by getting a software engineering-grade MacBook, but noted DOGE's lack of a centralized engineering playbook, leading to engineers starting from scratch. 
By Day 8, he realized DOGE functioned more like embedded McKinsey volunteers than a revolutionary force—with no direct authority, it was used as a

for unpopular decisions made by Trump-appointed agency heads. On Day 13, he attended a DOGE all-hands meeting (a candid Q&A with Elon), got approval to open-source his code (aligning with DOGE's transparency goal), and hosted the code on GitHub. 
From Day 21, he worked remotely in New York, focusing on two VA projects: modernizing VAGPT (VA's internal ChatGPT) to a mobile-responsive version with NextJS, Tailwind, and ShadCN; and developing an AI chatbot for VA.gov. On Day 34, he secured VA's approval to open-source VAGPT's repo and planned a May 19 hackathon for the chatbot. 
On Day 55, his access was revoked without warning after discussing his DOGE experience in a Fast Company interview (following a WIRED article revealing his role). He summarized his achievements: an LLM contract analysis tool, org chart visualizers, VAGPT modernization, a VA Chatbot demo, and accelerating VA's AI adoption by 12 months. However, he expressed disappointment at failing to ship products improving veterans' disability claims UX or processing speed—though VA ended a $380,000/month website contract by shifting work to an internal engineer. He concluded with gratitude for the experience and regret over unfulfilled goals.
summary_zh:
本文是萨希尔・拉文吉亚（Sahil Lavingia）以第一人称撰写的工作记录，讲述了 2025 年 3 月 17 日至 5 月 9 日期间，他作为软件工程师在美国退伍军人事务部（VA）下属的美国 DOGE 服务局（前身为美国数字服务局，于 2025 年 1 月经特朗普总统行政命令更名重组）的工作经历。
 入职前（第 0 天），他在曼哈顿 VA 医疗中心完成指纹采集，入职动机是希望为联邦政府编写代码 —— 这源于 2016 年他为伯尼・桑德斯竞选团队编写谷歌应用代码时，发现选民联络工作亟需更好的工具支持。
 第 1 天，他在华盛顿特区 VA 中央办公室宣誓就职，得知职位为参谋长高级顾问，薪资为 0 美元。首要任务是审查 VA 的 9 万多份有效合同（涵盖 IT 支持至景观维护）。针对合同审查手动操作繁琐的问题，他提出基于大语言模型（LLM）的解决方案：提取合同 PDF 文本、标记可取消合同、整理成 CSV 表格，并开发仪表盘供合同官员查看。
 第 2 天，他发现政府软件开发高度依赖 IBM、埃森哲等承包商，核心原因是政府笔记本电脑受安全政策限制，无法安装 Git、Python、Cursor 等必要工具 —— 解决该根本问题需国会干预，因此外包成为更实际的选择。
 第 3 天，他参与人员精简（RIF）计划会议，利用 VA 整合在 IBM HR Smart 中的 HR 数据，搭建了覆盖近 50 万员工的组织架构图可视化原型工具；同时了解到 RIF 规则极具强制性：优先考虑任职年限（新员工首当其冲）、退伍军人优先权，且资历高于绩效（与私营企业裁员侧重精简中层或低绩效员工形成鲜明对比）。
 后续他发现 VA 有多项重要项目（如将退伍军人福利申请处理时间从 133 天缩短至一周内）及开源实践（如已有 40 年历史的 VistA 电子健康记录系统）。在 VA 首席技术官（CTO）建议下，他通过配置软件工程级 MacBook 解决了笔记本电脑限制问题，但同时注意到 DOGE 缺乏集中化的工程操作手册，导致工程师需反复从零开始。
 第 8 天，他意识到 DOGE 更像嵌入各机构的麦肯锡志愿者，而非预期中的革新力量 ——DOGE 无直接决策权，常被特朗普任命的机构负责人当作不受欢迎决策的 “替罪羊”。第 13 天，他参加 DOGE 全员会议（实为与埃隆的坦诚问答会），获许将代码开源（符合 DOGE 透明度目标），并将代码托管至 GitHub。
 第 21 天起，他在纽约远程工作，专注两项 VA 项目：将 VA 内部 ChatGPT（VAGPT）升级为基于 NextJS、Tailwind、ShadCN 的移动端适配版本；为 VA.gov 开发 AI 聊天机器人。第 34 天，他推动 VAGPT 代码库开源获 VA 批准，并计划于 5 月 19 日举办聊天机器人黑客马拉松。
 第 55 天，在《连线》（WIRED）杂志披露其 DOGE 任职情况后，他又在《快公司》（Fast Company）采访中谈及工作经历，随后访问权限被突然撤销。他总结成果包括：LLM 合同分析工具、组织架构图可视化工具、VAGPT 升级、VA 聊天机器人演示版，以及将 VA 的 AI 应用进程加快 12 个月。但他也表达失望，因未能推出改善退伍军人残疾索赔用户体验或加快处理速度的产品 —— 不过 VA 终止了一份每月 38 万美元的网站修改合同，改由内部工程师每周花 10 小时完成。文末他对这段经历表示感激，同时对未实现的目标感到遗憾。
best_quote_en: “In the end, I learned a lot, and got to write some code for the federal government. For that, I'm grateful. But I'm also disappointed. I didn't make any progress on improving the UX of veterans' filing disability claims or automating/speeding up claims processing, like I had hoped to when I started. I built several prototypes, but was never able to get approval to ship anything to production that would actually improve American lives–while also saving money for the American taxpayer.”
best_quote_zh: “最终，我学到了很多，也得以为联邦政府编写一些代码。对此，我心怀感激。但我也感到失望。正如入职时所期望的那样，我在改善退伍军人提交残疾索赔的用户体验，或是实现索赔流程自动化、加快处理速度方面，没有取得任何进展。我搭建了几个原型，但始终未能获得批准，将任何真正能改善美国人生活 —— 同时为美国纳税人节省资金 —— 的产品投入生产。”

title: 1 张卡片生产力邪修法，简单粗暴，效率快
title_zh: 1 张卡片生产力邪修法，简单粗暴，效率快
summary_en:
This article introduces a minimalist productivity method called the 'One Card Productivity Evil Cultivation Method', which abandons complex efficiency software and relies solely on two paper cards to improve work efficiency. The term 'evil' refers to its counter-trend nature—it goes against the trend of pursuing various tools and instead uses simple paper cards to replace all productivity applications, emphasizing anti-complexity and anti-toolism. 'Cultivation' means it hones one's mindset: the limited space of a card forces people to prioritize and focus only on the most important tasks, shifting from 'time management' to facing key matters without evasion. 
The method requires only two paper cards: a 'Priority Card' and a 'Miscellaneous Task Card'. For the Priority Card, every morning, users write 1-2 most important, must-complete core tasks (e.g., writing an article, finishing a report) that require deep thinking and undisturbed focus. They then set a dedicated 'focus period' (e.g., 9-11 a.m.), during which only the Priority Card is kept on the desk and all communication tools are muted to avoid distractions. For the Miscellaneous Task Card, all trivial tasks (e.g., replying to emails, making calls, filling out expense reports) are written down. These tasks are handled only in gaps after completing Priority Card tasks or when energy levels drop, serving as a 'barrier-clearing list' to isolate trivialities from core work. 
The core principle of the method is 'isolation': the two cards are physically separated and never mixed. The Miscellaneous Task Card must not be visible when handling priority tasks, as this isolation protects the most scarce resource—focus. At the end of each day, both cards are discarded to eliminate historical burdens and procrastination lists, allowing users to start fresh the next day. 
The article explains why the method works: it is like 'hard cultivation' for productivity, restricting greed and self-deception by limiting space and time, which brings clarity and a sense of steadiness. It concludes by encouraging readers to try the method for a week, emphasizing that true productivity lies not in chasing more tools but in using the simplest way to do the most important things well.
summary_zh:
本文介绍了一种极简主义的生产力方法 ——“1 张卡片生产力邪修法”，该方法摒弃复杂的效率软件，仅依靠两张纸质卡片提升工作效率。其中 “邪” 体现在其反潮流属性：不追逐各类工具，反而用简单的纸质卡片替代所有生产力应用，强调反复杂、反工具主义；“修” 则指其对心性的磨炼：卡片的有限空间迫使人们取舍，只聚焦最重要的事，核心是从 “管理时间” 转向直面关键事务，不再逃避。
 该方法仅需准备两张纸质卡片：“要务卡” 与 “杂务卡”。“要务卡” 的用法是：每天早晨在卡片上写下当天最重要、必须完成的 1-2 个攻坚任务（如撰写一篇文章、完成一份报告），这类任务通常难度高、需深度思考且需不受干扰才能推进；随后设定 “专注时段”（如上午 9 点至 11 点），在此期间桌面只保留这张卡片，所有通讯工具全部静音，确保此时仅聚焦这一件事。“杂务卡” 则用于记录所有零碎事务（如回邮件、打电话、填报销、催进度），它并非核心工作战场，而是 “清障清单”，仅在完成 “要务卡” 任务的间隙或精力下降时集中处理，将琐事隔离出去本身就是一种解脱。
 方法的核心原则是 “隔离”：两张卡片物理隔离、绝不混合，处理要务时 “杂务卡” 绝不能出现在视野中，这种隔离并非形式，而是对最稀缺资源 —— 专注力的保护。每日结束时，两张卡片均随手扔掉，既让 “要务卡” 确保当天创造了真正价值，也让 “杂务卡” 清理了生活杂波，次日可轻装上阵，无历史包袱与拖延清单。
 文章解释该方法有效的原因：它如同生产力的 “苦修”，通过空间与时间的限制，约束贪心与自我欺骗，反而能让人体验到久违的清晰与踏实。文末鼓励读者尝试，建议坚持一周，强调真正的生产力不是追逐更多工具，而是用最简单的方式把最重要的事做到最好。
best_quote_en: “True productivity is not about chasing more tools, but about using the simplest way to do the most important things well.”
best_quote_zh: “真正的生产力，不是追逐更多工具，而是用最简单的方式，把最重要的事做到最好。”

title: How an invasive shrub derailed visions of prosperity in Kenya
title_zh: 一种入侵灌木如何让肯尼亚的繁荣愿景脱轨
summary_en: The article discusses how the introduction of the invasive shrub Prosopis juliflora (etirae), a species of mesquite, has negatively impacted Kenya's farmlands and local livelihoods, particularly in the arid north. Originally introduced in the 1980s by NGOs to combat famine and environmental issues like soil erosion and fuelwood shortages, the shrub was seen as a way to bring progress and stability to pastoralist communities. However, it has since become one of Africa's worst invasive species, outcompeting native flora, strangling farmlands, and causing health problems for livestock and people. The article uses this situation as a parable to argue that the 'visions of progress' held by development workers and policymakers are often rooted in a misunderstanding of local livelihoods and values. It contrasts the traditional, flexible pastoralist lifestyle with the rigid, predictable, and control-oriented idea of prosperity often imposed by external interventions. The author, Samuel F. Derbyshire, highlights through personal anecdotes and research that despite growing understanding of the dynamic nature of pastoralism, these old, flawed narratives persist. The piece concludes that genuine prosperity must be nurtured from the ground up, not imposed from the outside, and that the 'quiet work' of local people adapting to these challenges is often overlooked.
summary_zh: 这篇文章探讨了入侵灌木牧豆树（etirae）的引入如何对肯尼亚的农田和当地生计造成负面影响，尤其是在干旱的北部地区。该灌木最初由非政府组织在20世纪80年代引入，旨在应对饥荒和水土流失、燃料短缺等环境问题，被视为能为游牧社区带来进步和稳定。然而，它现已成为非洲最严重的入侵物种之一，挤占本地植物，扼杀农田，并对牲畜和人类健康造成问题。文章将这种情况比作一个寓言，指出发展工作者和政策制定者的“进步愿景”往往源于对当地生计和价值观的误解。它将传统的、灵活的游牧生活方式与外部干预强加的僵化、可预测和以控制为导向的繁荣理念进行对比。作者塞缪尔·F·德比郡（Samuel F. Derbyshire）通过个人轶事和研究强调，尽管人们对游牧生活的动态性质有了更深入的了解，但这些过时、有缺陷的叙事依然存在。文章总结道，真正的繁荣必须从基层培养，而不是从外部强加，而当地居民为适应这些挑战所做的“默默工作”常常被忽视。
best_quote_en: If Kenya's long entanglement with international development teaches us anything, it is that prosperity swells from the ground up. It must be nurtured, not imposed.
best_quote_zh: 如果肯尼亚长期与国际发展纠缠的经历教会我们什么，那就是繁荣是从基层兴起的。它必须被培养，而不是被强加。
